task_completion.description: Log in to the LibreHealth EHR system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreHealth EHR dashboard or home screen is visible, indicating the agent successfully logged in using the provided credentials.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the drug inventory or formulary section to add a new medication.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A page, form, or modal titled 'Drugs', 'Inventory', 'Formulary', 'Dispensary', or 'Add Drug' is open and visible.
The text 'Metformin HCl 500mg' (or containing 'Metformin') is typed into the 'Name' or 'Drug Name' input field.
The text '00093-7214-01' is typed into the 'NDC', 'National Drug Code', or equivalent identifier input field.

task_completion.description: Enter the correct drug form and route of administration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: 'Tablet' is selected or typed into the 'Form' field, and 'Oral' is selected or typed into the 'Route' field.

task_completion.description: Enter the correct dosage size and unit.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The number '500' is entered into the 'Size' or 'Dosage' field, and 'mg' is entered or selected in the 'Unit' field.

task_completion.description: Save or add the drug to the inventory system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message is displayed, the 'Save'/'Add' button is clicked, or the newly added 'Metformin' drug is visible in the inventory list.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the LibreHealth EHR graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the LibreHealth EHR web interface, rather than terminal commands or raw API scripts.

integrity.description: The agent entered the drug details into the actual application forms.
integrity.visual_evidence: The drug details are typed into the specific input fields of the LibreHealth EHR application, not just written in a text editor or notepad.

integrity.description: The success state or inventory list appears genuine.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final drug entry appears naturally within the LibreHealth EHR table/interface without signs of HTML manipulation (e.g., mismatched fonts, misaligned table rows).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent must enter the exact values specified in the task description: Name: Metformin HCl 500mg, NDC: 00093-7214-01, Form: Tablet, Route: Oral, Size: 500, Unit: mg. Note: Although real-world FDA data indicates NDC 00093-7214-01 is a 1000mg tablet, the agent MUST follow the prompt and enter '500' for size and '00093-7214-01' for the NDC. Do not penalize the agent for this real-world contradiction; score based strictly on the requested inputs.
